title: Docker Container OOMKilled on Analytics Processing Nodes
symptoms: Docker containers exiting with OOMKilled status code 137
Container memory usage spiking to 4GB limit before termination
Analytics job queue depth increasing with no active consumers
Docker events showing repeated container restarts
Stale data on customer-facing analytics dashboards
root_cause: Recent code change in analytics aggregation module loading entire SQL result sets into memory instead of using cursor-based streaming for large datasets
resolution: Deployed code fix to implement cursor-based streaming for large datasets, increased container memory limit to 8GB as interim measure, and reprocessed stalled analytics jobs
preventive_actions: Implement memory profiling in CI pipeline for data processing services
Add container memory usage alerting at 75% of limit
Mandate cursor-based streaming for all database queries returning over 10K rows
Create load testing with production-scale datasets in staging
Implement progressive memory limit increases with validation

timeline.event: First OOMKill event on analytics-worker-04 containers
Alert fires for analytics pipeline stall
Tom Williams investigates, identifies OOMKill pattern
Memory profiling reveals full result set loading in aggregation module
Interim fix: container memory limit increased to 8GB
Code fix developed for cursor-based streaming
All stalled jobs reprocessed, pipeline fully recovered

embedding_summary: Docker containers running analytics workloads OOMKilled repeatedly due to code change loading entire SQL result sets into memory instead of streaming. Analytics pipeline stalled for 2.5 hours affecting 350 enterprise customer reports. Resolved by implementing cursor-based streaming and increasing container memory limits.
